channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Teenager convicted for robbing tourist of luxury watch in Mayfair
item.description: In February 2024, Mohamed Sellaoui, a 17-year-old Algerian illegal immigrant, stalked and attacked a Chinese tourist in Mayfair, London, to steal a luxury Patek Philippe watch valued at £37,000. The v...

item.title: Urgent Measures Needed to Curb Kenya's Ballooning Public Debt
item.description: Kenya's public debt has become a big worry as it keeps shooting up, now standing at nearly 70% of GDP, way above what experts say is safe. The editorial calls on the government to take serious action ...

item.title: King Charles Discusses Alberta Separatism and Treaty Rights with Treaty 6 First Nations Leaders
item.description: King Charles met with a delegation of Treaty 6 First Nations leaders at Buckingham Palace on Wednesday, where discussions centred on Alberta's growing separatist movement and the protection of treaty ...

item.title: Diesel shortage and fuel price hikes hit gas stations and public vehicle drivers
item.description: Several gas stations in Metro Manila are facing diesel shortages following a recent surge in fuel prices. On the second day of the massive price hike, one station reported being out of diesel for two ...

item.title: Boots partners with Vitality to offer discounts on weight loss injections for active members
item.description: Boots has collaborated with health insurer Vitality to provide discounted weight loss injections, such as Mounjaro, through its Online Doctor service. This initiative targets Vitality members who earn...

item.title: Libina Lukumai strengthens finance and accountability at TEAGTL
item.description: At Tanzania East Africa Gateway Terminal Limited (TEAGTL), Libina Lukumai has become a central figure in ensuring financial integrity and operational efficiency. As the Collection Supervisor for Finan...

item.title: CBSE Class 12 Mathematics Exam 2026 Updates
item.description: The CBSE Board Exams for 2026 are currently underway, with students across India appearing for the Class 12 Mathematics paper today. While the original article intended to provide live updates for stu...

item.title: Jeremy Renner Denies Allegations of Involvement in Romance Scam Case
item.description: Marnie Reeves, a 56-year-old divorcée from Modesto, California, has filed a lawsuit alleging that Hollywood actor Jeremy Renner scammed her out of over $23,000 through what she claims was a romantic r...

item.title: Starmer warned of reputational risks from Mandelson's continued ties to Epstein before US ambassador appointment
item.description: Newly released government documents reveal that Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer received warnings about the potential reputational risks posed by Lord Peter Mandelson's ongoing relationship with the c...

item.title: SA government warns US ambassador over undiplomatic remarks
item.description: South Africa's International and Co-operation Minister Ronald Lamola has issued a warning to US Ambassador Leo Brent Bozell following a public speech in which Bozell criticised President Ramaphosa and...

item.title: Tina Wang Delivers an Emotionally Cohesive Piano Recital at Claremont United Church
item.description: On February 22, 2026, pianist Ching‑Ting (Tina) Wang presented a solo recital at the Sanctuary of Claremont United Church of Christ, focusing on a seamless emotional journey rather than separate showp...

item.title: Grammarly Discontinues AI Expert Review After Lawsuit Over Unauthorized Use of Names
item.description: Grammarly, owned by the tech company Superhuman, is facing a class action lawsuit after its AI-powered 'Expert Review' feature used the names and likenesses of authors, journalists, and other professi...

item.title: B.C. man misrepresented attendance at mandatory ethics course to regulator
item.description: A man in British Columbia arranged for a family member to attend a mandatory ethics course in his place, subsequently providing false information to the professional regulator about his own participat...

item.title: Call to Integrate Educational Theatre into Kenya's Competency-Based Education
item.description: In this letter to the editor, the writer passionately advocates for the inclusion of educational theatre in Kenya's Competency-Based Education (CBE) framework. They argue that theatre is a powerful to...

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Sees Multiple Attacks on Commercial Ships Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The situation in the Strait of Hormuz is worsening as Iran and Israel exchange heavy strikes, impacting commercial shipping and regional security. Recently, at least three ships, including vessels fla...

item.title: Former Vancouver Police Volunteer Facing Charges in Multiple Stranger Assaults
item.description: Records indicate that a former volunteer with the Vancouver Police Department was first charged last summer in connection with several assaults on strangers in Vancouver. The charges came after an inv...

item.title: Government Rolls Out P5,000 Cash Aid for PUV Drivers Starting with Metro Manila Tricycles Amid Oil Price Surge
item.description: The Marcos administration is set to provide financial assistance to public utility vehicle (PUV) drivers and operators because of sharp increases in oil prices. This surge is caused by the ongoing war...

item.title: Omar Abdullah Speaks on Attempted Attack on Father Farooq Abdullah
item.description: Omar Abdullah, former Chief Minister of Jammu and Kashmir, has shared details regarding an attempt on his father Farooq Abdullah's life. According to Omar, his father narrowly escaped serious harm dur...

item.title: Pentagon Limits Press Access Over Photos of Secretary Pete Hegseth
item.description: The Pentagon has reportedly restricted press access to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth due to concerns over 'unflattering' photographs. After a March 2 briefing—the first Hegseth attended since June 20...

item.title: Agriculture Drives South Africa's Economic Growth in 2025
item.description: The latest Statistics South Africa GDP figures have highlighted the agricultural sector as a key driver of economic stability. South Africa's GDP grew by 0.4% in the final quarter of 2025, resulting i...
